train_pairs.target: You had corn at home today.
Soon there will be no haricots in my pot.
Will there be a party in San Cristobal tomorrow?

train_pairs.source: 'Oy chan-vun ta batz'i k'op ta Jobel.
train_pairs.target: There is a Tzotzil school in San Cristobal.

train_pairs.source: Mi 'oy sbatz'i chi'il li Xun e?
train_pairs.target: Does Juan have a real friend?
I will soon have a good house.
Was there a school in San Cristobal last year?
Is there corn in his pot?
There will be good music at the party today.
What did you have at school yesterday?

train_pairs.source: Ch'abal 'ox schi'il li Romin e junabi.
train_pairs.target: Last year Domingo had no friend.

target: Was there music at the party?
Will there be music at the party?
What will there be in Mexico today?

test_pairs.source: "Oy "ox k'op ta batz'i k'op ta jna volje.
target: There was a talk in Tzotzil in my house yesterday.

verification.hypothesis:
A Mayan language!

My attempt at translation:
"The rain ruined the corn."

Breakdown:
- Ch'abal: rain
- alekil: ruined or destroyed
- 'ixim: corn

Please note that Mayan languages have many variations and dialects, so this translation might not be exact or universally accepted. If you have more context or information about the specific language or dialect, I'd be happy to try and refine the translation.
